0.response: {"Q0":"Not really, other than avoiding the planet(s) that would not produce sellable gems. For the lottery I went with intuition and comparing the different percentages vs the reward. Destroying the dirt/wood/stone was muscle memory after a few rounds. "}

7.response: {"Q0":"The difference of reward value and chance of getting each one. "}

9.response: {"Q0":"No. Me not remembering if the astronaut left for the game vs round was my fault, and it was just a slight doubt that I wanted to confirm. "}

1.response: {"Q0":"Only when the blue gems we no longer able to be sold. Then I stopped mining on the blue planet of course. "}

5.response: {"Q0":"Only once, as I got paranoid and second guessed myself if I remembered the rule correctly, haha. "}

6.response: {"Q0":"No. At that point I had confirmed the astronaut left for the rest of the game. "}

8.response: {"Q0":"Definitely selecting galaxies. It stressed me out more as it was more chance based, but I also had the planets buttons nailed down. So pressing the right buttons for each planet required no thought. "}

4.response: {"Q0":"Yes. Though I did second guess myself if it counted ONLY for the current round, or the rest of the game. To test that I only mined 1 gem from the blue planet to confirm I was correct, and it lasted the whole game. "}
